rationale: The proposed change increases a homestead exemption but does not abolish ad valorem taxes for school M&O, which is the sole trigger for §49.354 dissolution authority. No conflict.
quote: If the legislature abolishes ad valorem taxes for public school maintenance and operations and adopts another method of funding public education, the board of trustees of a consolidated district created under this subchapter may dissolve the consolidated district
_note: Increasing the homestead exemption amount does not abolish ad valorem taxes for school maintenance and operations, so the operative trigger in §49.354(a) is not implicated.
